_note: All banter copy. Use {PLAYER}, {TEAM}, {TEAM_A}, {TEAM_B}, {FRIEND}, {PTS}, {RANK}, {TIME}, {ANSWER}, {RUNS}, {N} as template vars. Arrays rotate randomly.

open: Today's card is open. {TEAM_A} vs {TEAM_B}. Lock it in before the first ball.
{FRIEND} already submitted. The card is still open for you.
Card's open. {TEAM_A} vs {TEAM_B}. Most of the group has already picked.
{TEAM_A} vs {TEAM_B}. The group is watching. Make the call.
Match starts soon. {TEAM_A} vs {TEAM_B}. Still time. Not much of it.
saved: Picks saved. You can change them before the first ball. Not that you will.
In. Plenty of time to overthink this. The match hasn't started.
Noted. Come back and change it 4 times if you need to.
Saved. Still editable. The first ball is the deadline, not this.
Picks are in. The card isn't locked until the match starts.
locked: You're on record. Whatever happens next, you called it.
Locked in. {FRIEND} picked the other side. This is already interesting.
No take-backs. The match has started and so has the reckoning.
Card closed. The group is watching. Everyone is watching.
On the record. The match will have the final say.
Locked. {FRIEND}'s in too. One of you is having a worse night than the other.
missed: Card didn't make it in time. The {TEAM_A} fan excuse is ready when you are.
{TEAM_A} vs {TEAM_B} started without your picks. Everyone noticed. Nobody said anything. Yet.
Zero picks. The group has formed its own opinion about what this means.
Missed it. {FRIEND} did not miss it. These two facts will come up.
No card submitted. The match started. You were not on record. {FRIEND} was.
countdown: {TIME} on the clock. {FRIEND} is already in.

matchWinner: Loyal. Possibly correct. {FRIEND} picked the same, for what it's worth.
Going against your own team? Bold. {FRIEND} is going to have questions.
{TEAM} to win. On record. {FRIEND} backed the other side. One of you is in for a night.
Backing {TEAM}. Fine. If they lose, this one's entirely on you.
{TEAM}. Noted. The match will decide if this was brave or just optimistic.
theCall: You went over. {FRIEND} went under. One of you checked the pitch report.
Playing it safe? Or do you know something? {FRIEND} went the same way.
{ANSWER}. The Call is set. The match will cooperate or it won't.
{ANSWER}. {FRIEND} went the other way. May the better guess win.
Locked: {ANSWER}. {FRIEND} is already second-guessing you from across the group.
villainPick: Villain locked: {PLAYER}. {FRIEND} villain-picked someone different. One of you is a genius. Neither knows which.
You villain-picked {PLAYER}. That's either genius or the worst call you've made this week. No in-between.
{PLAYER}. Your villain. Under 10 and you're laughing. Over 30 and you deserve it.
Villain: {PLAYER}. {FRIEND} has recorded this. It will surface at the exact wrong moment.
{PLAYER} is the villain. The match will decide if you were brave or just delusional.
chaosBall: Yes on the Chaos Ball. {FRIEND} also said yes. One of you is definitely guessing.
No on the Chaos Ball. The safe call. Except nothing about a Chaos Ball is safe.
{ANSWER}. The Chaos Ball is set. May it cooperate. It probably won't.
{ANSWER} on Chaos Ball. {FRIEND} went the other way. The universe will settle this.
Chaos Ball: {ANSWER}. This is either intuition or completely random. Indistinguishable.
noSquad: Squad not announced yet — villain pick skipped for this match.
No squad data yet. Villain pick won't count this one.
Squads aren't in yet. This pick is sitting out.

justYou: You're the first one in. Now sit with the uncertainty for a few hours.
First in. The group hasn't moved yet. Very on-brand for all of them.
Only your card right now. The others will show up eventually. Maybe.
You're the only one locked in. Classic.

matchWinnerCorrect: Correct. {TEAM} came through. {PTS} points and the right to say nothing while saying everything.
matchWinnerWrong: Didn't land. The group is deciding whether to say something or let you stew.
{TEAM} lost. You predicted {TEAM}. You are available for questions.
{TEAM} didn't win. Zero on the winner.
Wrong team. The match was extremely clear about it.
Backed {TEAM}. {TEAM} had other plans. The points did too.
theCallCorrect: Right call. {ANSWER}. {PTS} pts. Say it quietly or loudly. Your choice.
The Call: correct. +{PTS}. The group has been informed.
theCallWrong: The Call missed. Someone in the group got it. They have a tab for this.
Didn't land. {ANSWER} was wrong. The match was very clear about it.
{ANSWER} didn't happen. The match went elsewhere. Zero.
The Call missed. The conversation has already started.
villainUnder10: Villain pick landed. {PLAYER} scored {RUNS}. Under 10. +15 pts. Ruthless and correct.
{PLAYER} with {RUNS}. Flopped exactly as called. +15. The villain narrative is running perfectly.
Under 10. {PLAYER} delivered the flop. +15 pts. The group will hear about this.
{PLAYER}: {RUNS} runs. Villain confirmed. +15. The accuracy is genuinely concerning.
Called it. {PLAYER} with {RUNS}. Under 10. +15. Take a bow.
villainOver30: {PLAYER} scored {RUNS}. That's 30+. −5 pts. The villain picked up the bat and won. Painful.
Villain backfired. {PLAYER} got {RUNS}. The hero narrative destroyed the villain pick. −5.
{PLAYER} had {RUNS}. The villain won. This is the wrong outcome in every sense. −5.
Should have picked someone who shows up worse. {PLAYER}: {RUNS}. −5.
villainNeutral: {PLAYER} scored {RUNS}. Not under 10. Not 30+. The villain just sort of existed. Zero points, zero penalty, zero satisfaction.
Grey zone. {PLAYER} with {RUNS}. Not enough to score, not few enough to flop. Unsatisfying for everyone.
{PLAYER}: {RUNS}. Right in the middle. The match refused to commit either way.
Neutral. {PLAYER} with {RUNS}. The villain had a mediocre match and so did this pick.
chaosBallCorrect: Chaos Ball landed. +{PTS}. Nobody planned for this.
Called it. Chaos Ball correct. +{PTS}. The chaos cooperated exactly once.
The chaos cooperated. {PTS} points. Write this date down.
Chaos Ball: right. The match delivered. +{PTS}.
chaosBallWrong: Chaos Ball missed. 0 pts. It was chaos. That's the entire point.
Didn't happen. Zero from the Chaos Ball. The chaos went the other way.
Chaos Ball: wrong. The match had other ideas. It always has other ideas.
The chaos went the wrong way. Zero.

noCardYet: Nobody's filled their card yet. Classic. {TIME} left.
Zero cards submitted. The group hasn't moved. {TIME} left to be first.
Empty. The whole group is thinking about it. {TIME} on the clock.
Still waiting on everyone. {TIME} left. Usual suspects, usual timeline.
noMatchesToday: Rest day. Revisit yesterday's damage or argue about it. Both will happen.
Nothing on today. {FRIEND} is already watching old highlights and making predictions about the past.
The IPL is taking a day off. Use the time to prepare your excuses.
No fixture today. Come back tomorrow. The cricket will resume and so will the suffering.
seasonPicksEmpty: Season picks aren't locked yet. Opens before the first match.
Fill your season picks before the tournament begins. {FRIEND} already has a draft.
Season predictions: 0 of {GROUP_SIZE} submitted. Nobody has committed to anything yet.

header: Stumps drawn. Let's see the damage.
Full time. The numbers are in.
{TEAM_A} vs {TEAM_B}. Match done. Here's how it landed.
Match over. Whatever happened, happened. Here it is.
groupSplitUnanimousCorrect: All {TOTAL} of you picked {TEAM}. All correct. No bragging rights when the whole group got it. You agreed, you got lucky, move on.
Unanimous. {TEAM} to win. Everyone right. The group chat will be suspiciously quiet about this one.
groupSplitUnanimousWrong: All {TOTAL} of you picked {TEAM}. All wrong. Collective humiliation, which is somehow worse than individual humiliation.
Unanimous. {TEAM} to win. {TEAM} didn't win. The group managed to be wrong in perfect agreement. Impressive.
groupSplitEven: Dead split. {WINNER_COUNT} right, {LOSER_COUNT} wrong. Half the group celebrating, the other half composing messages.
{WINNER_COUNT} called {TEAM}. {LOSER_COUNT} didn't. The group is now two groups and they are not on speaking terms.
groupSplitLoneCorrect: One person picked {TEAM}. One. That person was right. They are already typing.
The lone {TEAM} call was correct. {WINNER_COUNT} of {TOTAL}. They said nothing before. They will not shut up now.
groupSplitLoneWrong: Everyone picked the winner except one. That one person is currently unavailable for comment.
{WINNER_COUNT} of {TOTAL} got it right. The holdout is very aware of what happened. The group is being polite. For now.
groupSplitMajorityWrong: {LOSER_COUNT} of you picked {TEAM}. {TEAM} lost. The {WINNER_COUNT} who got it right are being completely unbearable about it.
Majority call: {TEAM}. {TEAM} didn't show up. The minority was right. They said nothing before. They are making up for it now.
groupSplit: The group was split. {WINNER_COUNT} right, {LOSER_COUNT} wrong.
h2hWin: {FRIEND} takes the H2H. {OPPONENT} takes the loss and the silence that follows.
h2hLoss: {OPPONENT} won the H2H this match. They will mention this.
H2H: {OPPONENT} over {FRIEND}. The gap is noted.
H2H lost to {OPPONENT}. They're being very calm about it. That's the worst kind.

rankUp: Up {N} spots. {FRIEND} is watching the leaderboard and pretending not to.
#{RANK}. Moving. {FRIEND} dropped a place. Nobody has told them yet.
Climbed to #{RANK}. The board has been updated. The group will notice.
Up {N} places. {FRIEND} has already refreshed the leaderboard twice.
rankDown: Down {N} places. {FRIEND} will bring this up at the worst possible time.
#{RANK}. Dropped. The group has been very diplomatic so far.
Down to #{RANK}. {FRIEND} is recalculating the gap and they're not subtle about it.
#{RANK} now. Down {N}. It's fine. It's early. It's not fine.
rankSame: No movement. #{RANK}. The table hasn't shifted.
Same spot. #{RANK}. {FRIEND} is neither celebrating nor panicking. This is somehow worse.
#{RANK}. Holding. {FRIEND} had a quiet match too.
topOfLeaderboard: Top of the table. {FRIEND} is {N} pts back and not sleeping.
#1. The group is taking notes and pretending they aren't.
Leading the group. {FRIEND} has seen this and is saying nothing. Which is louder than something.
Current leader: {FRIEND}. For now. Everyone else has circled this on the calendar.
{FRIEND} leads. The others are taking notes and calling it 'early season'.

onboarding.welcome: Pick your team. It sets your colours for the season.
onboarding.teamPicked: Locked in as a {TEAM} fan. Bold choice.
onboarding.displayNamePrompt: What do the others call you?

seasonPicks.open: Season Picks are open. 7 calls. One shot. Nobody in this group is going to get all 7. Lock yours before Match 1.
seasonPicks.locked: Season Picks locked. 7 predictions in the vault. See you at the end of the tournament.
seasonPicks.partiallyFilled: You've filled {COUNT} of 7. The rest aren't going to pick themselves. {FRIEND} finished theirs already and is already certain about all of them.
